source_pr.title: planner: fix bug when unfolding wildcard in view definiton
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: guo-shaoge <shaoge1994@163.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #24933 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: When wildcard and subquery(e.g. select some_expr/agg/win_func from xxx) is used in view definition. We got unknown column error.

For example:

```
create view v as select * from (select count(*) from t1) s;
select * from v; // got error
create view v as select * from (select c1 or 0 from t1) s;
select * from v; // got error
```


**Why**: check the result of show create view. The `*` is unfolded to be `s.count(*)`, but in the subquery, the select field is `count(1)`. The column name is not same. (**PS**: This problem exists for other agg func/window func ...)

    SELECT `s`.`count(*)` AS `count(*)` FROM (SELECT COUNT(1) AS `count(*)` FROM `test`.`t1`) AS `s`

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed: **Add alias name for select filed when create view**. So the view definition will be:

    SELECT `s`.`count(*)` FROM (SELECT COUNT(1) AS `count(*)` FROM `test`.`t1`) AS `s`

How it works:
1. After `buildProjection()` in `buildSelect()`, we add alias name for each select field. The new added function assumes there is a **one-to-one mapping** between select fields and proj.OutputNames().

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->


- Integration test


Side effects: None

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- planner: fix bug when unfolding wildcard in view definiton
